narration: You're paying a professional to beat the market for you. Here's the thing — 92% of them can't. And the ones that did? They probably just got lucky.
visual: Dark background. Fund manager silhouette (person in suit with briefcase, red tie). Giant '92%' badge in red drops in below. Caption: 'Let's run the math.'

narration: Every year S&P publishes the SPIVA report tracking every active fund. After 15 years, 92% underperform a basic index fund. That's not a bad year — that's the industry.
visual: Light background. Two vertical bars: giant red bar labeled '92% ACTIVE FUNDS UNDERPERFORM', tiny green bar labeled '8% BEAT THE INDEX'. Red badge: 'OVER 15 YEARS'.

narration: Here's why. Active funds charge 1.2% a year in fees. Index funds charge 0.03%. That tiny gap costs you $180,000 on $100,000 over 30 years — just in fees.
visual: Dark background. Two growing bars: tall red bar '1.2% ACTIVE FUND', tiny green bar '0.03% INDEX FUND'. Green bordered box: '$180,000 difference'.

narration: And the managers who do beat the market? Vanguard studied it — only 1 in 4 top funds stay in the top the following year. That's not skill, it's a streak.
visual: Light background. Four piggy banks all green (top performers). Then three fade to grey, only one stays green. Labels: 'STAYS TOP' and 'DROPS OUT'.

narration: Run the real math. $10,000 in an average active fund over 30 years becomes $57,000. The same money in an S&P 500 index fund: $76,000. That $19,000 gap is pure fees.
visual: Dark background. Two growing bars side by side: red '$57,000 ACTIVE FUND', taller green '$76,000 INDEX FUND'. Green badge: '+$19,000 EXTRA'.

narration: The move most millionaires actually make isn't finding the right fund manager — it's not needing one. Total market index, low fee, set it and actually forget it.
visual: Light background. Three jars labeled 'US STOCKS 60%', 'INTL STOCKS 30%', 'BONDS 10%'. Green badge: 'LOW FEE. SET IT & FORGET IT.' CTA: 'Follow for more wealth math.'
